acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact or served dashboard response contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, [jj-omitted], configured secret values, or omission placeholders.
All docs, run artifact, manifest, log, planner, Codex event, diff, eval, and dashboard-visible writes are redacted before persistence.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.
jj serve can display intended docs and run artifacts through guarded paths only.
Command records include only sanitized argv/status/timing/result metadata and do not persist raw command text or raw environment dumps.
Manifest and dashboard security diagnostics expose only counts, booleans, root labels, relative-safe roots, sanitization status, dry-run parity status, and sanitized failure categories.
Denied path attempts are represented as sanitized categories and counts, not attacker-controlled path payloads.
Dry-run and non-dry-run apply identical redaction, path-boundary, and security diagnostic behavior.
jj serve dashboard links recent runs to guarded per-run detail pages.

A valid run detail page displays run status, timestamps, planner/provider metadata, dry-run mode, generated doc links, artifact availability, evaluation result, Codex result metadata, sanitized command metadata, disclosure-safe security diagnostics, and safe next-action hints when available.
Missing diagnostics render as unknown or unavailable states without errors or unsafe fallback fields.
Missing manifests, malformed manifests, missing artifacts, and older manifests without diagnostics render safe deterministic states without server errors.
jj serve dashboard links to a guarded run history page.
The guarded run history page lists discoverable runs newest first using sanitized manifest-derived fields only and links each row to the guarded run detail route.
Run history filters are allowlisted, deterministic, and do not reflect attacker-controlled invalid query values.
The run history page does not render raw secrets, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked path attempts cannot influence which run directories are listed or linked from run history.

Security behavior is covered by focused Go tests for redaction, path guards, artifact writing, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, dry-run and non-dry-run parity, diagnostics, serve access, run detail access, and run history access.
go test ./..., go vet ./..., and go build -o jj ./cmd/jj pass.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.
Keep all generated artifacts and workspace mutations inside the resolved target workspace and run artifact roots.
Prevent secrets, credentials, private key material, omission placeholders, and secret-looking environment values from being persisted or rendered.
Ensure jj serve provides a dashboard-first local experience without exposing arbitrary files or sensitive artifact content.
Preserve existing dry-run and non-dry-run workflows while applying redaction and boundary checks by default.
Expose useful disclosure-safe security diagnostics in manifests and dashboard views using counts, booleans, labels, relative-safe roots, and sanitized categories only.

Let users drill from the dashboard into guarded per-run detail pages that summarize run status, artifacts, evaluation, provider metadata, command metadata, Codex metadata, and safe health diagnostics.
Let users browse and filter guarded run history from jj serve without manually opening .jj/runs.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.
Add unrelated user-facing features while unresolved security risks remain.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads as diagnostics.
Add new security scanners or change redaction algorithms as part of run history or run detail features.
Add client-side full-text search over raw artifacts or manifests.
open_questions: Should explicitly provided plan files outside --cwd remain allowed as read-only inputs after canonicalization?
Should git diff artifacts be redacted in-place, supplemented with a redaction report, or both?
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using the documented path resolution behavior.
The resolved --cwd value, or process cwd when --cwd is absent, is the workspace boundary for generated docs, run artifacts, and workspace mutations.

Artifact writes, docs writes, manifest serialization, planner output, Codex output, command summaries, evaluation output, and dashboard render data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.
Path guards must canonicalize requested paths, evaluate symlinks where applicable, and reject traversal, absolute-path escape, encoded escape, and symlink escape outside allowed roots.
jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and .jj/runs roots and must reject traversal or symlink escape with deterministic 403 or 404 responses.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw environment values and raw or secret-bearing command text must not be stored.

Manifest security metadata may record secret presence booleans, redaction counts, denied path counts, guarded root labels or relative-safe roots, command sanitization status, dry-run parity status, and sanitized failure reasons, but never secret values.

Security diagnostics in manifests and dashboard-visible data must use counts, booleans, labels, relative-safe roots, and sanitized categories only, and must pass through shared redaction before persistence and before render.
Denied path attempts must be represented as sanitized categories and counts, not attacker-controlled path payloads.
Security failures must be actionable without exposing sensitive payloads.
OpenAI API planning, Codex CLI fallback planning, dry-run, non-dry-run, and dashboard flows must continue to work after redaction and boundary enforcement.
jj serve dashboard recent runs must link to guarded per-run detail pages.
jj serve run detail pages must resolve run IDs only under the guarded .jj/runs root, render sanitized manifest-derived metadata, and link to intended docs and artifacts only through guarded routes.

Run detail pages must display safe deterministic states for missing manifests, malformed manifests, missing artifacts, and older manifests without diagnostics, without raw path, command, env, secret, or payload disclosure.
jj serve must expose a guarded run history page that lists discoverable runs under the guarded .jj/runs root sorted newest first.
The guarded run history page must support allowlisted filters for run status, dry-run mode, planner provider, evaluation state, and run-id substring query without reflecting invalid raw query values.
Run history rows must be derived from sanitized manifest or dashboard DTO fields and link only to guarded run detail routes.
Missing, malformed, partial, and older manifests in run history must render as unknown or unavailable without server errors.
Traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked path attempts must not influence which run directories are listed or linked from run history.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a user planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security hardening remains a core constraint, and jj serve includes guarded per-run detail pages and a guarded run history browser for inspecting recent and older runs, filtering by safe manifest-derived fields, and reviewing artifacts, evaluation status, command metadata, Codex metadata, and disclosure-safe security diagnostics without opening raw manifest files manually.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
